title: ONNX: The PDF Format For Neural Networks
summary: ONNX is presented as a standardized format for neural networks, analogous to PDF for documents. It enables portable representation and deployment of trained models across different frameworks and platforms.
writeup: This video introduces ONNX (Open Neural Network Exchange) as a portable, framework-agnostic format for neural networks, analogous to PDF for documents. The through-line is a hands-on demonstration that a model trained in PyTorch and a functionally identical model defined in TensorFlow can both be exported to .onnx files and served through the same ONNX runtime inference code, with no PyTorch or TensorFlow required at runtime. The presenter also notes that Hugging Face hosts many models already in ONNX format, making deployment dependency-free without any training step.
key_points: ONNX was originally developed by Facebook and Microsoft and is now an open, non-proprietary format that stores the computation graph of a neural network independently of the framework that produced it.

Exporting from PyTorch requires passing dummy input data (e.g., a torch tensor) to torch.onnx.export alongside the model, the output filename, explicit input and output names, and dynamo=True to use the modern exporter.

Exporting from TensorFlow uses the separate tf2onnx package (TF2ONNX.convert.from_function) and does not require dummy data because the computation graph is fully specified by the input signature decorator.

The ONNX runtime inference code is identical regardless of whether the model originated in PyTorch or TensorFlow: create an ort.InferenceSession, read input and output names from session metadata, and call session.run with a numpy input array.
The presenter uses the Rust-based package manager UV (uv init, uv add) as a drop-in replacement for pip inside isolated environments, though standard pip install is equally valid.
Hugging Face hosts models pre-exported in ONNX format, allowing direct download and inference through the ONNX runtime without installing the original training framework.
